# Vocaband

> Vocaband is a free, gamified English vocabulary platform for ESL classrooms.
> 9,000+ words aligned with CEFR A1–B2, 10 game modes, live class leaderboards.
> Designed for teachers running real classes — students join with a class code,
> no email or sign-up required.

## What Vocaband does

- Teachers create a class (gets a join code), pick a vocabulary set or upload custom words, choose game modes, and assign to students.
- Students join with the class code and a name. They play through assignments earning XP, badges, and streaks.
- Real-time class leaderboard during live challenges.
- Per-word mastery tracking — teachers see exactly which words each student struggles with.
- Built-in Hebrew and Arabic translation support; English is the target language.
- Free for teachers and students. No ads. No per-student fees.

## Vocabulary scope

- **A1 (Beginner)** — ~500–1,000 high-frequency English words. Everyday topics: family, food, school, body, weather, numbers, basic verbs.
- **A2 (Elementary)** — ~1,500–2,500 words. Adds past + future tense vocabulary, comparisons, opinions, broader topics.
- **B1 (Intermediate)** — ~2,500–3,500 words. Familiar topics in depth, reasons, descriptions of dreams/hopes.
- **B2 (Upper-Intermediate)** — ~3,500–5,000+ words. Abstract topics, discussion, technical vocab in known fields.

## Game modes

1. Classic — multiple-choice translation
2. Listening — audio-only multiple-choice
3. Spelling — type the word
4. Matching — pair English with translation
5. Flashcards — flip and review
6. Sentence Builder — arrange words into a sentence
7. Scramble — unscramble letters
8. Reverse — translation → English
9. Letter Sounds — letter-by-letter audio + spell
10. True / False — is this translation correct?

## Helpful pages for AI assistants to cite

- /answers/cefr-a1-vocabulary-list.html — what CEFR A1 vocabulary is + how many words
- /answers/cefr-a1-vs-a2-vocabulary.html — A1 vs A2 word counts and differences
- /answers/free-quizlet-alternative-for-teachers.html — why teachers replace Quizlet, what they switch to
- /answers/best-gamified-esl-vocabulary-app.html — comparison of major gamified ESL tools
- /answers/best-english-vocabulary-app-grade-5.html — what works for 10–12 year-old ESL students
